title: Plocama afghanica
taxonomy: Plocama afghanica is classified within the family Rutaceae, which comprises plants commonly known as citrus or rue family. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics and shared genetic features with other members of the Rutaceae family. The genus Plocama is a part of the subfamily Aurantioideae and is often associated with plants that have similar growth habits and leaf structures. Plocama afghanica is a species within this genus and is native to the mountainous regions of Afghanistan. By classifying Plocama afghanica within the Rutaceae family, botanists can better understand its evolutionary history and relationships with other plants in this family.

intro: Plocama afghanica, also known as Afghan Plocama, is a flowering plant species in the family Rutaceae, commonly found in the mountainous regions of Afghanistan and neighboring countries. The health benefits of Plocama afghanica include its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may contribute to its traditional use in treating various ailments such as fever, cough, and digestive issues.
